meaning: adj.禁止通信的,被单独囚禁的
examples: The prisoner has been held incommunicado for more than a week.
Yesterday she was incommunicado, putting the finishing touches to her autobiography.
He was held incommunicado in prison for ten days before being released without charge.
For the next month or so, Mr. Bheda testified that he was held mostly incommunicado in police custody.
It said the prisoner was held incommunicado, receiving no visitors and without any contact with the outside world.
